title: centaurea resupinata subsp. degenii
intro: Centaurea resupinata subsp. degenii is a subspecies of the Centaurea resupinata plant, commonly known as a type of thistle, native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in traditional medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Centaurea resupinata subsp. degenii include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms of traditional herbal remedies.
